rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM T BURGESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to november 20, 2019. examiner not able to complete review. however, no later than noon on tuesday, november 5, 2019, petitioner must file an amendment to the petition that lists all fees previously awarded as liens against the real property (both attorney and conservator fees), and show the total currently due, including interest. the amendment must state the current FMV of the property and whether petitioner will also be seeking fees for his services as trustee of the trust. a file stamped copy of the amendment must be provided to Room 202 and POS of the NOH for the amendment must be filed no later than noon on wednesday, november 13, 2019.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/20/19. Court does not have a copy of the 4th Account. File stamped single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be dropped off at the Superior Court in room 202. (LRSF 2.7, 14.6). Court does not have a proposed order. A hard copy of the proposed order must be dropped off at the Superior Court in room 202. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARLYCE CONSTANCE CONNOR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Dismiss Probate
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) The absconding administrator distributed $72,408 to himself, without court order, and despite outstanding creditor's claims. 2) Administrator assigned $17,000 of his interest in this estate (as heir) in favor of Advance Inheritance LLC, which was paid off from escrow proceeds, without court order and despite there being outstanding creditors. It is unclear who authorized this payment. 3) The petition alleges that it is unknown whether all of Decedent's income taxes have been filed. Court to determine whether any additional action or notice should be required. 4) Attorney for administrator assigned $6,000 of her interest in statutory fees to Advance Inheritance LLC. She alleges that she personally satisfied the assignment.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NESTEL REVOCABLE TRUST, ESTABLISHED FEBRUARY 21, 1997 AND REVOKED ON JULY 1, 2019 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Seeking Court Order Determining The Validity Of Modification/Amendments To Trust Provision
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. the petition seeks a court order that the revocation of the third amendment and POA are valid, and that the Trustor has legal capacity to change her estate planning documents; objections filed by Mr. Fisher claim the allegations of breach of trust by Mr. Fisher are incorrect, and contain an accounting, which is not properly before the court; objections filed by Matther Nestel claim Trustor does not have capacity, and suggest using a professional fiduciary as trustee. The Court to take a first look at the pleadings and discuss resolution.
